items.title: Labor wins blue-ribbon seat of Morphett from Liberals

children.content: Liberal frontbencher Stephen Patterson loses his bid to be re-elected in the beachside seat of Morphett in the South Australian state election.

articles.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects

children.content: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

image.altText: A man with a blue shirt standing in a lane with other people behind

articles.title: Why isn't One Nation winning many seats in the SA election despite its high vote?

children.content: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?

image.altText: A person voting on a sample House of Representatives ballot paper, with names like Apple and Peach

gallery.altText: A bald man with glasses wearing a suit jacket and a purple shirt

thumbnail.altText: A bald man with glasses wearing a suit jacket and a purple shirt

items.title: Rural families consider giving up sport and school runs as fuel prices soar

children.content: Rural and remote families who are spending as much as $500 a week on fuel amid the current oil crisis say they are preparing to make tough decisions about whether to make school runs and to go without sport and even health care.

image.altText: Two primary school children outside their rural school.

items.title: Late wickets give South Australia edge in absorbing Shield final

children.content: South Australia strikes twice late on day four to snatch the momentum from Victoria in the Sheffield Shield final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

image.altText: Three South Australian players celebrate a Victorian wicket in the Sheffield Shield final.

items.title: Citizen scientists take to the coast to celebrate southern Australia's seaweed

children.content: South Australia's rugged south-east coast is known for its cold water smashing into limestone cliffs. But beneath the surface is a global hotspot of seaweed biodiversity.

image.altText: An underwater shot of colourful seaweed.

items.title: Kings one win away from NBL title after beating 36ers

children.content: The Sydney Kings edge a step closer to winning a sixth NBL championship following a 106-93 defeat of the Adelaide 36ers in front of a record crowd.

image.altText: Kendric Davis shoots during the NBL championship series between the Sydney Kings and Adelaide 36ers.

items.title: Annie is only 26, but will die within months. Here's what she wants you to know

children.content: Before ending her life through voluntary assisted dying, Annaliese Holland shares her final wishes and advice on living a full life.

gallery.altText: A young woman with long blonde hair wears a puffer jacket while sitting on a park bench, looking at the camera with a smile.
a young woman with long blond hair holds bright pink balloons in the shape of 26
a young woman is in a wedding dress in th emiddle of two women who go to kiss her on the cheek

items.title: City couple quit careers and take over outback pub

children.content: Burnt out from city life and careers in Brisbane, Liz Murphy and Norm Matau knew they couldn't keep going. So they left it all behind and started a new life as outback publicans.

image.altText: A man and woman stand behind a bar in a pub.

items.title: Demons overwhelm Blues in late MCG surge

children.content: The Demons complete an amazing comeback and pile pressure on Carlton coach Michael Voss, while West Coast celebrates an inspirational victory on the road against Port Adelaide.

image.altText: An excited Melbourne player roars in celebration after kicking a goal, as he is grabbed by a teammate.

items.title: Mass casualty exercise in Adelaide draws details from recent events

children.content: South Australian emergency services conduct their largest mass casuality exercise in history in a bid to test coordination between agencies.

gallery.altText: A unit with smoke coming from it on a footpath with people lying nearby.

Emergency service workers help people acting as patients.

items.title: Fans rethink driving to SA for AFL Gather Round as global fuel crisis continues

children.content: The SA government and AFL are in discussions to try to keep fans flocking to Adelaide for next month's AFL Gather Round, as the global fuel crisis threatens to disrupt travel plans.

image.altText: Multiple yellow signs reading 'pump not in use' attached to petrol pumps at a station

items.title: A century ago, a Liberal Party disintegrated, and there are lessons for its namesake

children.content: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

image.altText: A painting depicting a crowd in a street

items.title: Hatchet attack on dog at SA golf course prompts public appeal

children.content: A kelpie that was found with "horrific" head wounds at an Adelaide Hills golf course has prompted the RSPCA to issue a public appeal for information.
Warning: This story contains details some readers may find distressing.

image.altText: A dog with stitches in its head.

items.title: Where is Gus? Six months on, more questions than answers remain

children.content: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

image.altText: Composite image of missing boy Gus Lamont imposed on an image of the vast outback with a group of police.

items.title: Decisions made more than a decade ago are coming home to roost

children.content: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

image.altText: Towers at an oil refinery.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: As universities cut courses, spending on consultants and contractors nears $2b

children.content: Consultancies have been accused of infiltrating universities and wasting scarce public funds on questionable advice about cutting courses and jobs.

items.title: 'I feel betrayed': New scam figures show Australians lost more than $2 billion

children.content: Australians lost more than $2 billion to scammers in 2025, the latest data from the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has revealed.

image.altText: A man wearing a hoodie holds a laptop

items.title: Exmouth beaches a 'graveyard' after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

image.altText: A baby turtle lying dead on the sand surrounded by seaweed

items.title: Teen says single parent bias is leaving Australian families homeless

children.content: Hunter was plunged into homelessness when his family lost their rental. It is an increasingly similar story to other single parents finding themselves "lifelong renters".

gallery.altText: A young man smiles in front of branches.

thumbnail.altText: A young man smiles in front of branches.

items.title: Do you know who owns your GP clinic? The answer might surprise you

children.content: Major insurers like Medibank Private and Bupa are buying up GP clinics in a move that could lead to US-style managed care.

image.altText: A MyHealth medical sign

items.title: Kylie Minogue to lead AFL grand final entertainment, ending Aussie drought

children.content: Pop superstar Kylie Minogue has been named as the men's AFL grand final pre-match entertainment, the first time since 2021 that an Australian artist has headlined the event.

image.altText: Kylie Minogue sings with her arms in the air in a black and white dress.

items.title: Bondi nanny accused of torture for Pinochet makes last stand to evade extradition

children.content: Adriana Rivas has been a fugitive in Australia since fleeing Chile while on bail 20 years ago. A federal court hearing will determine whether she is removed to face trial for the alleged crimes of torture and kidnapping, committed on behalf of the Pinochet regime.

image.altText: Close up of a woman with dark, short hair and glasses.

items.title: Council meeting descends into disarray as councillors' faces obscured

children.content: The incident sparked a debate over conspiracies, technology, TikTok, and clout-chasing in Australia's largest local council.

image.altText: A blurry photo taken from a webcam in a virtual meeting

items.title: The best and worst things about being on reality TV, according to Guy Sebastian

children.content: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

items.title: How to discuss additional work-from-home days with your employer

children.content: With rising fuel prices adding additional financial stress to household living costs, what are your rights when it comes to working from home — and what are the best ways to approach your employer about flexible work arrangements?

children.content: Cast your mind back and see if you can conquer this week's round of trivia.

items.title: 'Perverse': Sydney council wants to ban commercial student housing

children.content: For every 10 student rooms being built, there is only one residential property in an area of Sydney's east. The mayor says the housing market has been "monopolised".

items.title: ASX to fall after Wall Street tanks, oil price pressure as Houthis enter war

children.content: Wall Street tumbled as the Middle East situation deteriorated further, and the ASX is set to be hit by the same wave of anxiety. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: 'Wholly and solely systemic racism': Anger over closure of Indigenous health hub

children.content: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

image.altText: A woman with glasses in front of an Indigenous artwork.

items.title: Farmers and fishers call for help with fuel prices, warn costs will be passed on

children.content: Farmers, fishers and business leaders are appealing to the federal and state governments to provide targeted relief to help them deal with massive fuel price increases caused by the Iran War, and the looming prospect of shortages.

items.title: 'Plenty of blue skies' forecast for most of Queensland over Easter weekend

children.content: Mostly sunny, cool Easter weather is expected for much of Queensland, with only brief coastal showers, while far-north areas such as Cairns can expect heavier, more persistent rain.

image.altText: joggers next to a sculpture

items.title: Liberals dump Moira Deeming from ticket for next state election

children.content: Controversial Victorian MP Moira Deeming has lost her preselection battle for the state election, with Liberal Party members replacing her with business leader Dinesh Gourisetty.

image.altText: Moira Deeming appears serious as she walks through the parliament carpark, wearing a purple blazer.

items.title: Quick hits: F1 driver limps from crash, teenager sets more records

children.content: Oliver Bearman limped away from a big crash after he needed to take evasive action. Here are the quick hits from the F1 Japanese Grand Prix.

items.title: Convenience stores selling illegal tobacco in Darwin spark organised crime fears

children.content: Convenience stores selling cheap, illegally packaged cigarettes have been popping up across Darwin and businesses are worried that organised crime and gang violence are next.

gallery.altText: Convenience store with red and black signage

items.title: Kerr and Carpenter hit the ground running for Chelsea in Asian Cup return

children.content: Sam Kerr scores and sets up another goal with the help of Matildas teammate Ellie Carpenter as the Australians return to London to help Chelsea take down Aston Villa.

image.altText: Sam Kerr jumps on Sjoeke Nusken's back. Ellie Carpenter celebrates behind them.

items.title: No transport relief for Sydneysiders as fuel prices surge

children.content: The NSW Liberals and the Rail, Tram and Bus Union (RTBU) have renewed calls for free public transport in response to surging fuel prices.

articles.title: Public transport to be free for Victorians in April as fuel prices climb

children.content: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

image.altText: A train stops at a station while commuters wait on the platform alongside it.

articles.title: Free public transport in Tasmania for three months to counter fuel crisis

children.content: The Tasmanian government says public transport will be free statewide for three months, starting on Monday, in response to surging fuel prices.
